correct_explanation: arithmetic dependency chains create long scoreboard stalls that prevent warp hiding
raw_response:
MEMORY_BOUND: NO

REASONING: Each thread performs only one global load and one global store, both fully coalesced, so memory traffic is very light at about 8 bytes per element. In contrast, the kernel does a fairly long chain of dependent floating-point arithmetic, including multiple divides, which increases instruction latency and arithmetic work per byte. That makes the kernel primarily compute/latency-bound rather than memory-bound.
parsed_reasoning: Each thread performs only one global load and one global store, both fully coalesced, so memory traffic is very light at about 8 bytes per element. In contrast, the kernel does a fairly long chain of dependent floating-point arithmetic, including multiple divides, which increases instruction latency and arithmetic work per byte. That makes the kernel primarily compute/latency-bound rather than memory-bound.
